title: Digital Marketing Fundamentals
description: Understand the core concepts of modern digital marketing. Learn how to reach customers online through search engines, social media, email, and paid advertising to build a comprehensive digital strategy.

seo_metadata.title: Digital Marketing Fundamentals Certificate | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Start your marketing career here. Learn SEO, social media, email marketing, and paid advertising fundamentals.

learning_outcomes: Define the core components of a digital marketing strategy
Understand the customer journey and marketing funnel
Differentiate between inbound and outbound marketing techniques
Identify key channels: SEO, SEM, Social Media, and Email
Measure marketing success using basic KPIs (ROI, CPA, CTR)

modules.title: The Marketing Foundation
modules.description: Core concepts and the customer journey.

lessons.title: Traditional vs. Digital Marketing
lessons.content:
The Paradigm Shift

Digital marketing allows for precise targeting, immediate measurable feedback, and two-way communication with consumers, unlike traditional broadcast marketing.

lessons.title: The Marketing Funnel
lessons.content:
Awareness to Advocacy

The funnel represents the customer journey: Awareness (top), Consideration (middle), Conversion (bottom), and finally, Loyalty and Advocacy. Different tactics apply to each stage.

lessons.title: Owned, Earned, and Paid Media
lessons.content:
The Media Mix

Owned media includes your website and email list. Earned media is PR and organic social shares. Paid media includes Google Ads and sponsored posts. A good strategy uses all three.

lessons.title: Inbound vs. Outbound
lessons.content:
Push vs. Pull

Outbound (Push) interrupts the user (e.g., display ads, cold emails). Inbound (Pull) attracts users who are already looking for solutions (e.g., SEO, content marketing).

lessons.title: Defining Marketing KPIs
lessons.content:
Metrics that Matter

Learn to track Customer Acquisition Cost (CAC), Return on Investment (ROI), Click-Through Rate (CTR), and Conversion Rate to evaluate campaign success.

lessons.title: Building a Strategy
lessons.content:
Putting It Together

A strategy requires defining a target audience (buyer personas), setting SMART goals, selecting the right channels, and allocating budget appropriately.

mcqs.question: What is a primary advantage of digital marketing over traditional marketing?

options.content: It allows for precise targeting and measurable results
It reaches people who don't use the internet

mcqs.question: In the marketing funnel, what is the stage where a user first hears about your brand?
A company's own website and email subscriber list are examples of:
Which of the following is an example of Inbound Marketing?

options.content: Buying an email list and sending a blast
Creating a helpful blog post that ranks on Google (SEO)

mcqs.question: What does ROI stand for in marketing?
Which metric tells you the percentage of people who clicked your ad after seeing it?
When a customer recommends your product to their friends, they are at which stage of the funnel?

options.content: The person who pays for the advertising budget
A semi-fictional representation of your ideal customer based on data and research
A robot used to buy ads automatically

mcqs.question: Search Engine Optimization (SEO) is primarily focused on:

options.content: Paying Google to appear at the top of results
Improving your website to rank higher in organic (unpaid) search results

mcqs.question: Which of the following is considered 'Paid Media'?

options.content: A viral tweet from a satisfied customer
A press release picked up by a news outlet
